description: Latency and on-device performance evaluation protocol for NepTTS-Bench. Defines metrics, thresholds, and hardware targets for streaming TTS inference — critical for the <500M parameter deployment goal.

notes.usage: Run the full latency benchmark on each hardware target. Measure time to first audio, real-time factor, peak memory, and chunk boundary quality. Report per-hardware-target results separately.
notes.evaluation: A passing system must meet 'pass' thresholds on the primary deployment target (A10G cloud). Mobile CPU thresholds are aspirational goals for on-device deployment. Chunk boundary quality ensures streaming doesn't degrade perceptual quality.
notes.reference: RTF and TTFA benchmarks calibrated against published streaming TTS systems (VITS, CosyVoice, MeloTTS).

time_to_first_audio.description: Time from input text submission to first audio chunk available for playback. Measures perceived responsiveness for streaming applications.
time_to_first_audio.measurement: Wall-clock time from model.generate() call to first audio chunk callback, excluding text normalization preprocessing

real_time_factor.description: Ratio of generation time to audio duration. RTF < 1.0 means the system generates audio faster than real-time. Lower is better.

peak_memory.description: Peak GPU/CPU memory usage during inference. Measured across the full test set to capture worst-case allocation.
peak_memory.measurement: Peak resident memory (GPU VRAM or CPU RAM) during inference, including model weights and intermediate buffers

chunk_boundary_quality.description: Perceptual quality difference between audio within streaming chunks and audio at chunk boundaries. Ensures that chunked streaming inference does not introduce audible artifacts at join points.
chunk_boundary_quality.measurement: Compute DNSMOS on 500ms windows centered on chunk boundaries vs 500ms windows at chunk midpoints. Report the delta.

a10g_cloud.description: NVIDIA A10G GPU — primary cloud deployment target
a10g_cloud.notes: Single-stream inference, no batching. Represents per-request latency.

mobile_cpu.description: Mobile CPU — aspirational on-device deployment target
mobile_cpu.hardware: Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 equivalent (or ARM Cortex-A78 class)
mobile_cpu.notes: On-device goal. Thresholds are aspirational — failing mobile thresholds does not disqualify the system.

test_set.description: 100-utterance evaluation set stratified by length for latency benchmarking.
test_set.sampling: Randomly sampled from the full evaluation corpus, stratified by character count. Include at least 10 utterances with English code-switching.

procedure: Load model and run 5 warmup utterances (discard timings)
For each of the 100 test utterances, run 3 measured inference passes
Record per-utterance: TTFA, total generation time, audio duration, peak memory
Compute RTF = generation_time / audio_duration for each utterance
For streaming mode: record per-chunk generation times and run DNSMOS on boundary windows
Report median and p95 for all latency metrics (median for typical, p95 for tail)

aggregate_reporting.description: How to report latency and performance results.
visualizations: TTFA distribution histogram per hardware target
RTF vs utterance length scatter plot
